description_long: This house features a complex, multi-winged layout with intersecting gabled and hipped roofs, supported by visible wooden trusses and beams. The structure is primarily composed of a light-colored masonry or concrete base, with a prominent framework of timber rafters forming the roof skeleton. Large, open window spaces are distributed throughout the exterior, suggesting ample natural light and a modern, airy interior. The central section of the house is flanked by two substantial wings, each with its own roofline and chimney, while a covered breezeway or entry connects the main volumes. The roof structure is partially transparent or unfinished, revealing the intricate geometry of the supporting framework. The overall design blends traditional residential forms with contemporary openness and structural expression.
description: A large, multi-winged house with intersecting gabled and hipped roofs, exposed timber trusses, and a masonry base. The structure features open window spaces and a central connecting entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, central courtyard, and the intersecting rooflines with visible timber trusses.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the wings, the extended roof overhangs, and the prominent chimneys.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open breezeway, symmetrical wings, and the exposed roof framework.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the central section, roof geometry, and the open window placements.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large open sections
attribute_entryway_design: Central covered entryway with a breezeway, wide steps, and minimal decorative elements
